overview.description: The BondBloxx BB-Rated USD High Yield Corporate Bond ETF (XBB) is an exchange-traded fund that mostly invests in high yield fixed income. The fund tracks a market value-weighted index that specifically invests in USD-denominated corporate bonds with a credit rating of BB (or its equivalent), based on an average of three main credit rating providers. XBB was launched on May 24, 2022 and is issued by BondBloxx.
infoTable: ICE BofA BB US Cash Pay High Yield Constrained Index

data.title: ETF Edge: Managing long-term risk amid a new Fed chair nominee, jobs data and market volatility
data.text: From a new Fed chair nominee to a hard turn to risk-off trading in some parts of the market - the broader landscape is rapidly changing for ETF investors. BondBloxx ETFs co-founder Joanna Gallegos and...

data.title: Focus on intermediate credit ETFs, says BondBloxx ETFs co-founder Joanna Gallegos
data.text: Joanna Gallegos, BondBloxx ETFs co-founder and COO, joins CNBC's Dominic Chu on 'ETF Edge'  to discuss how investors should position in bonds following the nominee of Kevin Warsh to Fed chair.

data.title: Don't buy bonds, buy ETFs: Bondbloxx's Joanna Gallegos
data.text: Joanna Gallegos, BondBloxx Investment Management co-founder, and Sarat Sethi, DCLA managing partner, join 'Power Lunch' to discuss buying corporate debt.
